title: Bring up LilyGO LoRa radio path
description: Why this exists: the selected LilyGO board is only useful as a mesh target if the LoRa radio path works with correct SPI, DIO, reset, busy, RF switch, TCXO, antenna, and regional parameters. What needs to be done: validate Zephyr radio binding, SPI bus, GPIOs, interrupts, reset sequencing, SX126x/SX127x variant, RF switch/TCXO control, TX power constraints, receive path, and LICHEN link-layer integration. Acceptance criteria: firmware can initialize radio; TX smoke test succeeds; RX or peer exchange succeeds with known-good device or simulator bridge; failures create specific follow-up Beads with captured logs and pin/component evidence.
notes: EC2 build evidence: launched temporary us-east-2a builder i-0a15fbfd587d937fe with EBS cache vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted at /mnt/lichen-zephyr; initialized isolated workspace from lichen/west.yml; applied local T-Deck gateway HAL patch; ran 'west build -p always -b t_deck/esp32s3/procpu lichen/apps/gateway -d build/t_deck_gateway_h7t575'. Build passed and generated ESP32-S3 image. Generated config evidence: CONFIG_LICHEN_HAL=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_BOARD_NAME="LilyGO T-Deck", CONFIG_LICHEN_HAS_LORA=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_RADIO_MODEL_SX126X=y, CONFIG_LORA_SX126X=y, CONFIG_SLIP is not set, CONFIG_BT is not set. DTS evidence: chosen zephyr,lora=&lora0; t_deck_tft_cs_hog and t_deck_sd_cs_hog present; SX1262 dio2-tx-enable, dio3-tcxo-voltage=<0x2>, tcxo-power-startup-delay-ms=<0x5>. Memory: FLASH 213996 B / 8388352 B (2.55%), iram0_0_seg 51484 B / 329 KB (15.28%), dram0_0_seg 92552 B / 313 KB (28.88%), irom0_0_seg 110986 B / 8 MB (1.32%), drom0_0_seg 148460 B / 8 MB (1.77%). Artifacts: zephyr.bin 209K, zephyr.elf 5.4M. Physical radio TX/RX smoke still blocked because no T-Deck/Espressif USB device is visible locally; do not close until hardware logs or peer exchange evidence exists. EC2 cleanup complete: sync, unmounted /mnt/lichen-zephyr, detached volume and waited available, terminated instance, deleted temp security group and keypair.
